boundaryRules: Domain/application modules should not depend on infrastructure or API contracts unless explicitly transitional and documented.
EngineeringWorkflow module application contracts/services should use AssistantEngineer.Modules.EngineeringWorkflow.Application.* namespaces.
Module-boundary dependency direction is governed by docs/architecture/module-boundary-matrix.json and enforced by architecture tests.
Protected endpoint authorization gate remains a stable facade; P8-03A characterization now freezes decision semantics and anti-enumeration outcomes before extraction.
EngineeringWorkflow controllers remain API adapters; workflow shell route/signature/status/response behavior is characterized in P8-03D before staged migration.
EngineeringWorkflow diagnostics/state/submission orchestration helpers are migrated to module application ownership in P8-03E while API keeps composition-root wiring.
EngineeringWorkflow main controller shell uses an API-local action service for validate/prepare orchestration after P8-03F extraction while preserving route/signature/status behavior.
OwnershipBackfill tooling must remain outside runtime API execution path.
OwnershipBackfill CLI parser uses command-descriptor catalog and shared argument reader after P8-04 while preserving command/help/exit/redaction/apply-disabled semantics.
Scripts/tools rationalization in P8-06 classifies wrapper/tool/workflow boundaries and keeps release wrapper semantics stable.
Terminology/claims wording for architecture and security governance is normalized by docs/architecture/terminology-and-claims-vocabulary.json in P8-07.
Governance test brittleness reduction in P8-08 adds shared semantic assertion helpers while preserving strict behavior-level guardrails.
Final P8 closure decision and deferred P9 backlog are documented by docs/architecture/p8-engineering-domain-hardening-closure.json.
Release scripts/tools must not introduce runtime write-path behavior.
Calculation physics logic must remain in runtime C# modules with behavior-locked tests.
Route inventory classification closure in P8-05 reduces unknown classifications and tightens ignore-list coverage without changing controller/runtime behavior.
knownBoundaryRisks: Workflow and authorization hotspots are staged in P8-03; P8-03A/B/C gate characterization and collaborator extraction, P8-03D workflow controller shell behavior characterization, P8-03E helper migration, and P8-03F main-shell reduction are complete with behavior-lock constraints preserved.
OwnershipBackfill command-specific parse branches remain concentrated in parser internals and can be optionally split further in later tooling stages.
Infrastructure-to-module dependency surface remains broad and should be reviewed for minimization in later stages.
Controller read-history/report-artifact partial shells remain broader than desired and are candidates for optional follow-up decomposition.
Route inventory still contains staged Deferred entries (authorization rollout backlog) even after P8-05 unknown-classification closure and ignore-list tightening.
Some historical docs still contain repeated status-note blocks and remain candidates for optional P9 deduplication.
nonClaims: No external standard-case validation completion claim.
No DB RLS/global EF query filter claim.
